prd.title: Windows Port — Cross-Platform Expansion to Windows 10/11 x86_64

stories.title: Spike — Identify Zed commit with stable DirectX 11 Windows backend
Bump GPUI pin + validate Linux/macOS regression
stories.resolution: no-op — current pin 0b984b5 already 8 months post-DX11 (PR #34374, 2025-07-30). Evidence + AC disposition in tasks/spike-windows-build.md § US-002 resolution addendum.

stories.title: Provision windows-2022 CI runner with WiX + VS Build Tools
Cfg-guard POSIX process kill + add Windows TerminateProcess path
Platform-neutral symlink in install_method.rs
stories.resolution: No runtime symlink creators exist; two test-only sites gated on cfg(unix) per AC-6 (skip symlink creation on Windows). Breadcrumb comments flag future runtime implementers to use symlink_file + copy fallback per AC-1/AC-2.

stories.title: Windows stubs for workspace port scan + cwd_now
stories.resolution: Verification-only: existing cfg(not(any(target_os = linux, target_os = macos))) stubs at workspace.rs:427 (detect_ports → vec![]) and terminal.rs:817 (cwd_now → None) already cover Windows. Comments strengthened with design-choice language for US-022 to transpose into docs/WINDOWS.md. Zero runtime change.

stories.title: Migrate ipc.rs to interprocess crate + runtime_paths Windows branch
Initialize WiX config (packaging/wix + Cargo.toml metadata)
stories.resolution: packaging/wix/{main.wxs,License.rtf,paneflow.ico} committed (cargo-wix 0.3.9 seed, WiX v3 namespace). src-app/Cargo.toml [package.metadata.wix] declares wxs path + stable upgrade-guid/path-guid. Follow-up for US-015/US-016: cargo-wix 0.3.x generates WiX v3 XML but US-003 CI installs WiX v5 via `dotnet tool install --global wix` — version mismatch needs resolution at build-wiring time.

stories.title: Azure Trusted Signing onboarding + secret provisioning
stories.resolution: Playbook authored at memory/project_windows_signing.md (AC-5 ✓ + AC-6 fallback ✓). Azure Portal actions AC-1..AC-4 (account, cert profile, service principal, 6 GH Secrets) pending Arthur + Microsoft business-verification SLA (days to 6 weeks). Memory file contains the literal secret names US-015/US-016 consume; those stories can proceed with the names in place and fail at runtime if the secrets aren't yet populated. If onboarding exceeds 6 weeks, reopen with `resolution: fallback-to-OV-cert` per AC-6 and spawn a Sectigo OV cert procurement story.

stories.title: scripts/sign-windows.ps1 signtool wrapper
stories.resolution: scripts/sign-windows.ps1 authored (316 LOC). Implements canonical signtool invocation (/fd SHA256 /tr <server> /td SHA256 /dlib <dll> /dmdf <json> <msi>) with 3-host timestamp retry (acs.microsoft.com -> digicert -> sectigo), metadata.json generation with ExcludeCredentials (prevents managed-identity hang), signtool verify + Get-AuthenticodeSignature self-checks with O=Strivex anchor. NuGet Microsoft.ArtifactSigning.Client pinned to 1.0.128. AC-5 (missing env vars) and input-validation paths verified on pwsh 7.4 via container; AC-2/AC-3/AC-6 runtime paths deferred to US-016 CI dry-run (require Windows + populated Azure secrets). Memory/project_windows_signing.md updated with rebrand note (Trusted Signing -> Artifact Signing).

stories.title: MSI build + sign steps in release.yml
stories.resolution: release.yml gains 5 Windows-gated dormant steps (152 LOC insertion): preflight WiX v3, install cargo-wix 0.3.9 (pinned, --locked), Produce MSI (cargo wix build --no-build --package paneflow-app --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --install-version ${TAG#v}), Sign MSI (pwsh -File scripts/sign-windows.ps1 with all 6 Azure secrets + semver glob guard), Stage Windows assets (rename to paneflow-<ver>-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.msi + sha256 sidecar, nullglob array). Mirrors the dormant-macOS precedent (lines 448-522); activates when US-017 adds the windows-2022 matrix entry. AC-4 (continue-on-error: true on matrix entry) is called out in an inline block-comment for the US-017 implementer — not in scope for US-016. signtool preflight removed because sign-windows.ps1 self-resolves (review feedback: avoid false-confidence dual resolution). cargo-wix re-used existing Swatinem/rust-cache (~/.cargo/bin). actionlint + shellcheck clean on all added lines.

stories.title: release.yml 5th matrix leg (windows-2022)
stories.resolution: Matrix expanded to 5 entries — added `{target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, arch: x86_64, runs-on: windows-2022, continue-on-error: true}` after the Intel Mac entry. Activates the Windows-gated steps from US-016 (preflight WiX → cargo-wix install → Produce MSI → Sign MSI → Stage Windows assets). Best-effort parity with Intel Mac: the continue-on-error flag lifts via the pre-existing expression at release.yml line 21. Also corrected the release job's atomic-release comment (was imprecise about fail-fast interaction with continue-on-error legs). AC-5 (cargo build <20/35 min) + AC-6 (dry-run produces signed MSI) measurable only on first real tagged release with US-014's Azure secrets populated. actionlint clean, matrix count verified (5 entries).

stories.title: ci.yml windows-check job
stories.resolution: Promoted US-003's windows-check smoke job to a full PR quality gate. Three edits to ci.yml: (1) added `components: rustfmt, clippy` to dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable step, (2) inserted cargo fmt --all --check + cargo clippy --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc -- -D warnings + cargo test --workspace --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc in AC-2's exact order before the pre-existing cargo check step, (3) removed `continue-on-error: true` from cargo check so the gate is required. Comment block updated to reflect post-EP-W2/W3 state. Linux `check` and `macos-check` jobs untouched (AC-6 verified via `git diff --unified=0` showing a single hunk at line 200+). US-003's WiX install/verify steps kept — removing would regress US-003 AC-3 and wall-clock impact (~5-10s cached) is inside AC-4's <5 min budget (estimated ~2.5 min cached total). AC-5 (POSIX regression → file:line error) verified by inspection: clippy `-D warnings` + cargo check without continue-on-error would fail PR on any `use std::os::unix::*` addition. actionlint clean.

stories.title: Winget manifest files + update-winget.yml bot
stories.resolution: Four new files authored. 3 manifest templates in packaging/winget/ at ManifestVersion 1.12.0 (installer.yaml with wix InstallerType + UpgradeBehavior: uninstallPrevious; locale.en-US.yaml with Publisher=Strivex + License=MIT + LicenseUrl + Tags; version manifest) using __VERSION__/__URL__/__SHA256__ sed placeholders (verified by local dry-run sed rendering + schema field checks). update-winget.yml (~220 lines) triggers on release:published + workflow_dispatch, resolves strict-semver tag (rejects pre-releases), downloads MSI via gh release download, computes uppercase-hex SHA-256, sed-renders to /tmp/rendered-manifests/, clones fork ArthurDEV44/winget-pkgs, syncs upstream master, branches paneflow-<ver>, commits, force-pushes, opens PR to microsoft/winget-pkgs via gh pr create with idempotency check (gh pr list --head). Inline credential helper pattern (single-quoted) keeps WINGET_PAT value out of argv — mirrors update-cask.yml. Pre-merge prereqs: (1) fork microsoft/winget-pkgs under ArthurDEV44, (2) add LICENSE file at repo root (project-wide gap per CLAUDE.md), (3) WINGET_PAT secret (classic PAT, public_repo scope), (4) first submission manual. AC-4/AC-5 verification deferred to first real release.

stories.title: Website download page Windows column
stories.resolution: Single-file edit to paneflow-site/src/components/download/download-view.tsx: (1) extended DownloadItem to support href-or-copyText plus optional ComponentType icon, (2) new DownloadRow component handling link-vs-copy rendering with promise-guarded clipboard write + 2s Copy→Check icon swap + useRef-backed timer cancellation on re-click/unmount, (3) new windowsItems(version) yielding MSI link (paneflow-<ver>-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.msi matching US-016 AC-3) + winget install copy button, (4) wired into the existing Windows column (removed placeholder), (5) headline updated 'Linux' → 'Linux et Windows'. Linux row visual appearance preserved (leading icon gated behind item.icon set). bun run build clean, 5 static pages generated. All 5 ACs verified live via Chrome MCP: MSI href matches expected URL, clipboard write captures exact winget command, icon swap Copy→Check→Copy fires correctly on happy path, Promise rejection keeps Copy icon (no false success), 3-column grid stacks vertically at 553px viewport with no horizontal overflow. install.tsx has the same (pre-existing) clipboard pattern without the Promise/timer hardening — out of scope for US-020 but noted as a follow-up.

stories.title: Windows 10 1809 + Windows 11 smoke test suite
stories.resolution: docs/WINDOWS-SMOKE-TEST.md authored (417 lines). Structure mirrors docs/validation-aarch64.md (sibling aarch64 runbook). 10 scenarios per PRD AC-1 verbatim: (1) fresh MSI install, (2) SmartScreen Strivex, (3) Start Menu launch, (4) Ctrl+Shift+N workspace, (5) Ctrl+Shift+D split, (6) PowerShell 7 spawn, (7) 100 chars + resize ConPTY stability, (8) Ctrl+C interrupt (alacritty#3075 caveat documented), (9) close+reopen config persistence, (10) uninstall %APPDATA% preservation. Each scenario has a [📸] marker (10 total, mapped to smoke/<tag>/win{10,11}-<N>-<slug>.png naming convention per AC-5). AC-4 triage: 3-bucket workflow (fix-in-PRD / known-v1 / block-release) with concrete story-ID examples per bucket + default-to-C tie-breaker. AC-2/AC-3 (actual Win 10 1809 + Win 11 runs) deferred to first real release. Review verdict APPROVE_WITH_NITS; 5 of 7 findings applied — skipped: WiX casing check (already correct in main.wxs:%ProgramFiles%\PaneFlow\) and run-log note template (stylistic).

stories.title: docs/WINDOWS.md — known upstream risks + user guide
stories.resolution: Three deliverables. (1) docs/WINDOWS.md (226 lines) with the 5 required sections: Supported versions (x86_64 only v1, 10 1809+, 11), Installation (winget + direct MSI), Known limitations (services sidebar, cmd.exe OSC 7, CJK IME caution, cwd_now stub, self-update prompt-only), Known upstream risks (5 entries — IME CJK panic zed#12563, ConPTY Ctrl+C alacritty#3075, RDP broken zed#26692, devcontainer freeze zed#49072, GPU NoSupportedDeviceFound zed#28683 — each with title/description/URL/severity/workaround per AC-2), Reporting issues. (2) README.md +43 lines: new ## Windows subsection after macOS mirroring the sibling's structure, with two links to docs/WINDOWS.md (AC-3). (3) .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/windows-bug-report.md (114 lines) paralleling the existing aarch64-bug-report.md with a REQUIRED 'Windows version + build' field (winver + systeminfo guidance) + architecture/CPU/GPU/install-format/display-environment fields (AC-5). AC-5 deviation from PRD's literal 'bug_report.yml' filename: project convention is markdown templates (not YAML forms) — matched aarch64 template. AC-4 no-site-duplication decision documented in docs/WINDOWS.md preamble. Review verdict APPROVE_WITH_NITS; single SHOULD_FIX applied (display-text-vs-href consistency on WINDOWS-SMOKE-TEST.md cross-reference). Install path casing PaneFlow matches packaging/wix/main.wxs Directory Name; publisher Strivex matches sign-windows.ps1 O=Strivex anchor.
